title: Design Patterns
purpose: Recognize reusable design structures while avoiding unnecessary abstraction.
outcomes: Explain Design Patterns concepts from first principles
Choose when to use and when to avoid important Design Patterns features
Implement, test and debug practical Design Patterns solutions
Answer conceptual, internal-working and production interview questions

stages.title: Fundamentals
stages.objective: Build an accurate mental model of Design Patterns before relying on frameworks or memorized answers.

topics.title: Pattern intent and tradeoffs
topics.definition: Pattern intent and tradeoffs is a fundamentals part of Design Patterns concerned with how engineers reason precisely about computation, resources and system behavior. It provides a vocabulary, operating model and constraints for making that part of a system deliberate rather than accidental.
topics.whyItMatters: Without understanding Pattern intent and tradeoffs, a Design Patterns solution may work on the happy path but become difficult to change, debug or operate. This topic makes ownership, behavior and failure conditions explicit.
whenToUse: Use Pattern intent and tradeoffs when it directly supports a required Design Patterns behavior
Use it after identifying the input, output, constraints and failure policy
Use it when the team can test and operate the resulting behavior
whenNotToUse: Do not add Pattern intent and tradeoffs only because it is popular or appears in an architecture diagram
Avoid it when a simpler model satisfies the actual constraints
Avoid abstraction before the repeated problem and ownership boundary are understood
internalWorking: Input enters the Pattern intent and tradeoffs boundary and is validated against its contract
Invariants and representations determine correctness, execution and complexity
State changes should preserve a named invariant and produce an observable result
Failures propagate through a defined error, retry or recovery path
tradeoffs: Correctness and clarity versus implementation effort
Runtime performance versus memory or operational complexity
Flexibility versus a smaller, easier-to-test contract
commonMistakes: Memorizing the definition of Pattern intent and tradeoffs without tracing an example
Ignoring invalid input, failure and cleanup behavior
Choosing an advanced implementation before measuring the simpler one
topics.practicalExample: In the placement platform, apply Pattern intent and tradeoffs to one Design Patterns workflow such as creating an assessment, loading study content or calculating results. Define the contract, implement the happy path, reject invalid input and record enough context to diagnose failure.
